name: VisiData (Terminal Data Explorer)

performance.notes: Lazy loading; handles millions of rows with async loading; Python overhead for transforms

agent_readiness.notes: Terminal spreadsheet for exploring tabular data. Supports CSV, TSV, Excel, JSON, NDJSON, HDF5, SQLite, Postgres, S3, HTTP, zip/tar. Interactive TUI: sort, filter, aggregate, join, pivot, plot histograms. Frequency tables, statistics. Edit cells and save. Python expressions for column transforms. Macro recording. Extensible with plugins. Not designed for scripting — interactive exploration tool. Can output results via cmdline flags.

gotchas: Interactive TUI — not designed for pipeline scripting
Python speed limits performance on very large files vs xsv/miller
best_when: Interactive exploration of CSV/JSON/Excel data in terminal
Quick data profiling without writing code
avoid_when: Very large files requiring high performance
summary: VisiData is a terminal spreadsheet / TUI for interactively exploring tabular data. Supports CSV, JSON, Excel, SQLite, HDF5, S3, and databases. Sort, filter, aggregate, pivot, plot histograms, join sheets. Python expression transforms. Macro recording. Best for interactive ad-hoc data exploration at the command line — not designed for scripting. 'vim for data' — high learning curve, very powerful once learned.
